steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The first part is an independent clause: Okie-Tex takes place in an area with low light pollution. The second part, starting at the blank, is a participial phrase (ending with “dark skies…”). A participial phrase after an independent clause requires a present participle with no comma or additional pronoun.

steps.title: Apply the rule for participial phrases
steps.content: Choice A (ensuring) is the only present participle that correctly links the independent clause to the participial phrase. Choices B and D add unnecessary pronouns (“this” and “it”), while choice C uses a third-person singular verb form that creates a run-on sentence. That matches choice A — A is correct.
